mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly job growth above 100k signals robust labor market conditions that boost consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 100k monthly growth.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP and industrial production indicates strong aggregate demand, creating positive momentum in equity markets over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 and INDPRO both rise > 1% sequentially
failure_condition: The joint growth of GDPC1 and INDPRO fails to generate positive 60d SPX returns in 50%+ of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation combined with low unemployment signals central bank policy constraint, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns during late-cycle monetary tightening regimes.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3% and UNRATE is below 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals improved supply chain efficiency and corporate capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX fails to produce positive 5d returns in 50%+ of instances where INDPRO increases by 1.5%+ sequentially.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts indicate elevated demand for residential construction, boosting related sectors and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where HOUST exceeds 150k monthly growth.

mechanism_narrative: Rising personal consumption expenditures (RSAFS) combined with strong job growth (PAYEMS) indicates robust consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and PAYEMS both rise > 1% sequentially
failure_condition: The joint growth of RSAFS and PAYEMS fails to generate positive 20d SPX returns in 50%+ of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the federal funds rate above 4% signals aggressive monetary tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns during rate-hike cycles.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in 60%+ of instances where FEDFUNDS increases by 0.5pp+ monthly.
